posts.name: Soren R Nielsen
posts.cooked:
I spotted these lonely burgs among a lot of old private cellared Bordeaux, sat for sale in a local shop.
It’s a Danish bottled Gev. Chamb. from Maison Doudet-Naudin.
Never had a wine from this producer. Their records go back to 1849, and they are still using the same caves and premisses. The Doudet’s acquired the domaine in 1933, and has run it since. Not a famous producer of Grand crus, but still they have 15 appellations on their 13 ha. -Mostly village and 1’er crus.




They are bottled by a wine store (Kjær & Sommerfeldt) still existing, and situated 700 meters from My place. I know the staff well, and if “their” bottles are flawed, I can always go there and complain about it…?

At $100 a bottle, it’s a gamble I will take again and again.
I left one bottle though, as the fill was some lower.

Any Doudet-Naudin, or 1962-burg, infos are welcomed.

Regards, Soren.

posts.name: John Gilman
posts.cooked:
Hi Soren,

The bottles look really lovely- particularly the color of the wines through the glass. I do not have any experience with Doudet-Naudin in this era, but 1962 is a really fine, fine vintage for red Burgundy. Throughout the decade of the 1990s, this was probably the very finest drinking vintage of the decade of the 1960s (which was very long on outstanding red Burgundy years, as '61, '62, '64, '66 and '69 are all first class vintages). It is my impression that the '62s in general have lost just a touch of steam in the last decade, but let’s say that they are getting to the far side of their plateau of peak maturity, rather than in decline, and the ones I have drunk in the last year or two have been immensely satisfying and still quite vibrant. I think you will be very happy with these two bottles, particularly given the price and the lovely ruby color your photos show.

All the Best,

John

posts.name: Peter Chiu
posts.cooked:
SAQ ( Montreal, Quebec, Canada ) carries his Burgundy wines. Latest release of one of his product is : a 2014 Pinot Noir - but made from grapes from Languedoc and is at CA$16.95.

My friend Stephane loves this wine and drunk 3 already. I bought one but did not have a chance to try it…yet !!



*Doudet Naudin Pinot Noir 2014
Vin rouge, 750 ml
Code SAQ : 12898823 Code CUP : 03660600002711
France
Producteur M. Doudet-Naudin
Degré d’alcool 12,5 %
Le pinot noir n’a plus aucun secret pour cette maison implantée en Bourgogne depuis 1849. Présentée sous la classification Vin de France, cette cuvée fruitée et florale est élaborée quant à elle pour sublimer ce cépage bourguignon sur les terroirs du Languedoc.

posts.name: Matthew Hemming
posts.cooked: Actually a mate of mine owns the negociant side of Doudet Naudin these days. The family have the vineyards but my friend has first refusal on their distribution. They’ve a deep cellar of old wine. Last time I was there we drank 1956 Batard amongst others.

posts.name: Andy B
posts.cooked: I’ve drunk Doudet-Naudin library releases from the 40s and 50s that have been good. In the last year or two I stumbled upon a '29 Chambertin and '29 Clos Vougeot. Color looks good. Planning on opening the Chambertin later this year.

posts.name: Soren R Nielsen
posts.cooked:
Library releases. I never see these in Denmark. Is it via auctions or importers, or ?
Happy to learn that Doudet Naudin is a skilled name, capable of making great wine, that ages well. Now I wish for some fresher vintages, sometimes hard to find everything retail in Denmark. But on the lookout now.

Thanks for answers.

-Soren.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: 53 Corton from D-N was pretty good, one bottle left in my cellar

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Doudet-Naudin English bottlings from the '60s have been more than solid in my experience…

posts.name: Richard Albert
posts.cooked:
Sounds and like a great $100 gamble.
Skal!

posts.name: Matthew Hemming
posts.cooked: Am pretty sure a lot of older Berry Bros Burgundies were from D-N. There are quite a lot of parallels between Doudet and Remoissenet.

posts.name: Robert Pavlovich
posts.cooked: Maybe this will give some hope to those who’ve grown impatient with their 95’s and 96’s
